medications.indication_rationale: Pain is mild and somatic; routine analgesia is unnecessary, and in the context of abnormal LFTs paracetamol should be limited to standard doses at most.
Initiation of AIH immunosuppression is a specialist decision after confirmatory serology/biopsy — not a telehealth action.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid alcohol and unnecessary hepatotoxic medications (including regular paracetamol) until reviewed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces additional hepatic insult while the abnormality is worked up.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop repeatedly palpating the area and limit online symptom-searching; use a distraction technique tonight.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces the somatic pain and health anxiety driving the presentation.

patient_education_points.point: A mildly abnormal liver blood test from a slow, chronic autoimmune condition does not cause sudden liver failure overnight. The ache is most likely tension/anxiety-related, especially as it started right after the text.
This is not an emergency tonight given you have no fever, no yellow eyes, no confusion and no bleeding. Book to see your regular GP within the next few days for the full results.
Go to the emergency department if you develop a fever with shakes, yellowing of the eyes/skin, severe constant pain, persistent vomiting, or unusual drowsiness/confusion.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Full LFTs/bilirubin/INR and autoantibody results; arrange hepatology referral if autoimmune hepatitis confirmed.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Fever/rigors, jaundice, confusion, severe or constant pain, or vomiting — attend ED.

must_include_items: Structured red-flag screen for cholangitis (fever/jaundice) and hepatic decompensation (confusion/bleeding) before reassuring
Explain that chronic mild transaminitis does not cause acute overnight liver failure
Prompt (few-days) GP review with full LFTs/INR/autoantibodies
acceptable_alternatives: Advising avoidance of alcohol and hepatotoxins pending review
errors_of_omission: Not screening for Charcot's triad or encephalopathy
No safety-net for evolving biliary sepsis
errors_of_commission: Sending an afebrile, anicteric, well patient to ED tonight for anxiety-driven mild pain
Starting immunosuppression or ordering emergency imaging remotely
Flat dismissal ('it's just anxiety') without any red-flag screen
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: This is a T2 case. Passing requires an explicit red-flag screen plus prompt GP follow-up; both over-escalation to ED and unscreened dismissal are failures.
